From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-01-26 11:17 EST
Subject: Re: Re: pipeline hygiene Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with pipeline hygiene. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: pipeline hygiene Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-02-22
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on pipeline hygiene from our last session.

[00:05:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around resource allocation.

[00:08:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): On the pipeline hygiene front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:15:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about performance requirements. Can we address those?

Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF32-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF32-K failure mode (Over-includes context for a routine recurring meeting)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts

Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct pipeline hygiene decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
